meaning: n.喧嚣v.絮絮不休地说,喧闹
examples: She shouted over the growing din.
The din from the commodity pits on the Chicago exchanges is growing louder.
"Quiet, you scugs," he cried, "or I'll cast anchor in you." And at once the din was hushed.
The speaker cuts through the din to beam messages to the captain's ears only.
Just like other top athletes, tennis players can apparently concentrate amid the din of the crowd.
